items.title: CMS guidance: limits on federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens start Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS issued guidance on implementing a law that restricts federal matching funds for full Medicaid and CHIP benefits for certain lawfully present noncitizens. Beginning Oct. 1, 2026, federal matching funds for full Medicaid and CHIP benefits will be restricted to legal permanent residents, certain Cuban and Haitian immigrants, and Compact of Free Association migrants, while emergency Medicaid federal matching remains available.

items.title: HUD changes EHV and Stability Voucher verification rules; PHAs must comply within 60 days
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-05 removes a prior waiver that had allowed some Emergency Housing Voucher (EHV) and Stability Voucher (SV) admissions without first verifying SSNs and eligible noncitizen status. The notice says PHAs must be in compliance with eligible immigration status verification for new SV admissions and current EHV/SV families within 60 days of publication, and must verify SSNs in EIV or collect verification within the same 60-day period.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: HUD makes $10 million available for HUD-VASH administrative fees; applications due March 2, 2026
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-01 (issued Jan. 15, 2026) announces $10 million in additional administrative fee funding for public housing agencies administering HUD-VASH vouchers. HUD says applications must be received no later than midnight on March 2, 2026 (in the PHA’s time zone), and the funds are limited to eligible HUD-VASH administrative and related expenses.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC cash-value benefit amounts; states must allow at least one non-fresh fruit/veg form by April 2026
items.summary: USDA FNS says WIC state agencies must authorize at least one form of fruits and vegetables beyond fresh (frozen, canned, and/or dried) by April 2026. For FY 2026, USDA lists monthly cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts of $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants, reflecting an 11.8% inflation adjustment over FY 2022 baseline amounts.

items.title: SSA: 2.8% COLA for 2026; increases start for SSI on Dec. 31, 2025 and Social Security in Jan. 2026
items.summary: SSA says Social Security and SSI benefits will increase 2.8% in 2026, affecting 75 million Americans. SSA says increased payments start for SSI recipients on Dec. 31, 2025 and for nearly 71 million Social Security beneficiaries in January 2026.
